description: Positions vector B so its tail is at vector A's tip, creating a tail-to-tip chaining effect. The result is vector B repositioned and can be used in further operations.
utility: Chain one 3D vector to another in tail-to-tip configuration.

arguments.name: vecA
arguments.description: The anchor vector whose tip defines the chain point

arguments.name: vecB
arguments.description: The vector to chain, repositioned so its tail is at vecA's tip

examples.expression: G = g3d(at(4,20), view(20, 20)); VA = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0); VB = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0); VC = chain3d(VA, VB)

examples.scenario: Build a vector sum using chaining

examples.expression: G = g3d(at(4,20), view(20, 20)); V1 = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0); V2 = forward3d(V1); V3 = chain3d(V1, V2)
